title: Coelogyne demissa
taxonomy: Coelogyne demissa is classified under the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the genus Coelogyne, which consists of over 200 species of terrestrial and epiphytic orchids. The species Coelogyne demissa is native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. This orchid is further categorized into the subgenus Coelogyne, which includes many species of Coelogyne orchids. The classification of Coelogyne demissa reflects its unique characteristics and its position within the orchid family.

intro: Coelogyne demissa, also known as the "Hidden Coelogyne," is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia, characterized by its unique, white and greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne demissa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as 5-methoxy-7-hydroxy-2-(3,4,5-trihydroxyphenyl)-4H-1-benzopyran-4-one, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelogyne demissa is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health conditions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic properties.
